title: Converting Scheme Code with Macros to Haskell Code with Type Classes
scenario: A user wants to convert a complex Scheme code with macros into equivalent Haskell code with type classes. The Haskell code must be readable, maintainable, and efficient. The user needs help on how to define the Haskell functions and handle macros such as type introspection. The target Haskell code must be accurate and well-documented.
constraints: Must use the latest Haskell syntax
Must include a brief explanation of the conversion process

evaluationCriteria.description: The accuracy of the Scheme to Haskell code translation
The readability and maintainability of the Haskell code
The accuracy and correctness of the Haskell type classes used
The quality and clarity of the documentation provided
